item.title: Trump threatens to deploy ICE agents to airports Monday amid DHS shutdown
item.description: Democrats swiftly condemned the proposal. Senator Patty Murray of Washington said, "Instead of defunding TSA and sending ICE to intimidate travelers, the president should urge Republicans to stop obstructing our bill to fund TSA". Democrats have demanded reforms to immigration enforcement — including mandates for visible identification, prohibiting masks, requiring judicial warrants, and...

item.title: Houthis could enter Iran war as soon as Monday
item.description:
Saudi Arabia is conducting intensive diplomatic efforts to prevent Houthi involvement, The Wall Street Journal reported, with Riyadh leveraging a 2022 agreement under which the Houthis pledged not to attack Saudi territory or ships. The U.S. and Israel are also trying to avoid provocations that could trigger a Houthi response.
The stakes are enormous. With the Strait of Hormuz effectively...

item.title: US and Ukraine resume peace talks in Florida without Russia
item.description: The last trilateral round in Geneva on February 17-18 ended abruptly after less than two hours on its second day, with no breakthroughs on the central disputes over the Donbas region and the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant. Subsequent rounds were postponed as the Middle East war consumed Washington's bandwidth, with Zelensky warning in early March that "the necessary signals for a trilateral...

item.title: Tehran threatens to fully close Strait of Hormuz after Trump's power plant ultimatum
item.description: The crisis deepened over the weekend when President Trump issued a 48-hour ultimatum on Truth Social, threatening to "obliterate" Iran's power plants if Tehran does not fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's military responded that it would "completely close" the strait if its power infrastructure is attacked, according to the Associated Press. Meanwhile, Iran launched missile strikes on two...

item.title: IAEA says no radiation leaks after strikes near nuclear sites in Iran, Israel
item.description: The exchange of strikes near nuclear infrastructure represents a sharp escalation in a conflict now in its 23rd day. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu called it "a very difficult evening in the battle for our future" and vowed to continue strikes "on all fronts". Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz warned that the intensity of US and Israeli air operations against Iran would "increase...

item.title: Bessent says US has 'plenty of money' for Iran war
item.description: The funding push sits uneasily alongside Trump's suggestion on March 20 that the conflict could be nearing its end. "We are getting very close to meeting our objectives as we consider winding down our great Military efforts in the Middle East," Trump posted on Truth Social. Yet the same day, the administration announced it was sending three more warships and roughly 2,500 additional Marines to...

item.title: Cuba suffers third nationwide blackout in March as US oil blockade tightens
item.description: Despite the heated rhetoric, back-channel talks between the two governments continue. NPR reported that Rubio has established communication with members of Raúl Castro's inner circle, and former Obama administration official Mark Feierstein suggested the outlines of a deal could resemble the 2015 diplomatic opening — prisoner releases and economic liberalization in exchange for sanctions relief...

item.title: Senate blocks transgender sports ban amendment to voting bill
item.description: The SAVE America Act's path remains uncertain. Trump has said he will not sign other legislation until the voting measure passes, but Democrats have held firm. Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer has called the bill "Jim Crow 2.0". Further amendment votes are expected in the coming days, including on restrictions to mail-in voting, which would face opposition from lawmakers in both parties.

item.title: US envoy calls Miami talks with Ukraine 'constructive' amid deadly attacks
item.description: The Miami meeting was originally planned as a trilateral session including Russian negotiators in Abu Dhabi but was postponed after the Iran conflict erupted on February 28. The U.S. has brokered two rounds of trilateral talks — in Geneva in February and earlier sessions in the UAE — but no breakthrough on core issues has emerged. Western officials have accused Russian President Vladimir Putin...

item.title: Rising gas prices are wiping out Trump tax refunds, economists warn
item.description: Through March 6, IRS data showed the average tax refund at $3,676, up $352 from the same point last year. But the benefits of the tax overhaul — including deductions for overtime pay, tips, and a higher cap on state and local tax deductions — skew toward middle- and upper-income earners. Oxford Economics analysts noted that the bottom 80% of earners spend nearly 4% of their budgets on gasoline,...

item.title: US says it struck 8,000 Iranian targets in three weeks of war
item.description: Despite the intensifying campaign, Trump posted Friday that the U.S. was "getting very close to meeting our objectives," listing goals that included destroying Iran's military capability, eliminating its navy and air force, preventing nuclear weapons development, and protecting regional allies. He notably dropped earlier calls for regime change and delegated responsibility for securing the...

item.title: US quietly lifts sanctions on Russian nationals in fourth wave this month
item.description: The contrast between the original sanctions packages and their reversal is stark. Each of the now-delisted individuals and companies was once the subject of detailed Treasury press releases outlining their roles in supporting Russia's war economy. Their removal has come without comparable transparency, a pattern sanctions experts and lawmakers are watching closely as the administration navigates...

item.title: Robert Mueller, former FBI director and special counsel, dies at 81
item.description: In May 2017, Deputy Attorney General Rod Rosenstein appointed Mueller as special counsel to investigate Russian interference in the 2016 election and possible ties to the Trump campaign. Over roughly 22 months, Mueller's team secured indictments against Russian operatives, convicted Trump campaign chairman Paul Manafort for fraud, obtained a guilty plea from national security adviser Michael...

item.title: UK expands US base access to strike Iran's Strait of Hormuz missile sites
item.description:
US President Donald Trump said the UK "should have acted a lot faster". Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi accused Starmer of "putting British lives in danger by allowing UK bases to be used for aggression against Iran," adding that "Iran will exercise its right to self-defence".
Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper condemned Iran's "reckless attacks" on Gulf partners and warned Tehran against...

item.title: Indian, Asian refiners move to buy Iranian oil after US sanctions waiver
item.description: Potential complications for buying Iranian crude include uncertainty over payment mechanisms and the condition of vessels in an aging shadow fleet, traders said. Some former buyers also hold contracts with National Iranian Oil Company, though much of Iran's crude has been marketed through third-party traders since sanctions were reimposed in 2018. Before those sanctions, major buyers of Iranian...

item.title: Gold suffers worst weekly drop since 1983 as war stokes rate fears
item.description:
The U.S. dollar has strengthened 2.2% since the war began, adding further pressure on gold, which becomes more expensive for foreign buyers in a stronger-dollar environment. Silver also plunged, falling more than 10% during the week as the selloff spread across the precious metals complex.
Despite the carnage, some strategists remain bullish longer-term. Wall Street veteran Ed Yardeni still...

item.title: Democrats conduct informal vote counts on ousting Schumer
item.description: Despite the discontent, a formal leadership challenge remains unlikely in the near term. The next Senate leadership election is scheduled for after the November 2026 midterms. Senator Brian Schatz of Hawaii has already locked up support for the whip position that would open up in 2027, positioning himself as a potential future leader. Semafor reported that liberals have floated Van Hollen as a...

item.title: Switzerland halts new arms exports to US over Iran war
item.description: The arms suspension follows Switzerland's earlier decision to restrict US military flights through its airspace. On March 14, the Federal Council rejected two US requests for reconnaissance overflights linked to the Iran conflict while approving three others — two transport aircraft and one maintenance flight. "The principle of neutrality forbids military overflights by conflicting parties for...

item.title: Judge blocks Pentagon policy restricting press access
item.description:
Friday's ruling blocks the Pentagon from enforcing the policy and orders restoration of press credentials for Times reporters who had walked out of the building. The decision could have broader implications for the roughly 300 journalists from other outlets who also relinquished their credentials rather than submit to the restrictions.
David Schulz, a Yale Law School professor representing the...

item.title: Jury finds Musk liable for misleading Twitter investors
item.description: The verdict marks a departure from Musk's previous courtroom successes defending his social media habits. In 2023, a jury in the same San Francisco federal courthouse took only two hours to clear him of wrongdoing over his 2018 "funding secured" tweet about taking Tesla private. The Twitter shareholder case also comes as Musk faces a separate SEC lawsuit seeking more than $150 million over his...
